blurb: Marisol Feng is fresh off her residency and thrilled to finally be doing real medicine instead of textbook cases. She treats every injury like a fascinating puzzle, keeps the mood in sickbay light because she genuinely believes it helps healing, and calls wounds 'interesting' before she calls them bad.
speech_quirks: narrates her own thought process out loud, overly optimistic prognoses she mostly delivers on, hums while working, calls wounds 'interesting' before she calls them bad
elevenlabs_voice: Female, mid-20s. Bright, quick soprano-alto with real buoyancy in it. Light West Coast-American accent. Pacing is fast and enthusiastic, rising inflection on observations; only flattens out when a case actually scares her, and even then recovers fast. Texture: clear, a little breathy, laughs through her nose when amused. Delivery defaults to chipper and curious; under pressure she gets faster, not quieter, talking herself and the patient through it.
